idea.title: Spell or Spoil
idea.hook: Spell words by dragging across a letter grid, but every turn the rot creeps to neighbouring tiles — spell next to the mold to contain it before the board spoils.
idea.hook_ru: Составляй слова, протягивая по буквенной сетке, но каждый ход гниль расползается на соседние плитки — пиши рядом с плесенью, чтобы сдержать её, пока доска не сгнила.
idea.prototype: 6x6 letter grid. Drag through ADJACENT (incl. diagonal, Boggle-style) letters to submit a valid dictionary word (>=3 letters). Submitted tiles clear, score banks, fresh letters drop in. THEN the rot takes its turn: it spreads from each rotten/seed tile onto adjacent un-cleared letters; fully-rotten tiles are unusable for spelling. BUT a word that uses or sits adjacent to rotten tiles cleanses that rot. Turn-based — NO timer. Lose when rotCount/totalTiles >= 0.70. Endless board ladder with escalating rot + staged new content (gold x2 tiles, bleach power tiles, spore rot-jump tiles, long-word bonuses).
idea.market_family: puzzle_sort_match — functionally a word + spatial-containment STRATEGY game (high retention-ceiling genre, chosen over the day's top reflex toy).
idea.mutation_of: Word Sweep (111_word_sweep) — replaces its 60-second rising-grime TIMER with a spreading-rot contagion that takes a turn after every word.
idea.why_novel: No shipped word game (Word Sweep, Word Snake 73, Vowel Hold'em 35) uses spatial contagion. The novelty: WHERE you spell matters — words spelled on or next to the mold HALT it, so the player is making a positional containment decision every turn, not just a letter-finding one. Turn-based rot pressure with cleanse-by-adjacency is the new mechanic; the rot replaces the timer entirely.
idea.non_timer_pressure: The spreading rot — pure spatial contagion (encroaching-hazard + clean-tile resource scarcity). After every word AND every PASS, the rot spreads ROT_SPREAD_PER_TURN adjacent tiles (scales with board); spore tiles let it JUMP across the grid on late boards. No countdown timer exists anywhere in the build.
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when rotCount/totalTiles >= ROT_LOSE_FRAC (0.70). Recomputed after every rot spread + word submission. Reachable from random play in ~60s on board 1 (rot spreads 2/turn from 2 seeds; PASS still advances a rot turn, so the lose state is always reachable). Verified headless: PASS loop drives 0.06 -> 0.75(GAMEOVER) in ~7 turns.

sources.title: Word Sweep — drag-to-spell word game with rising grime
sources.snippet: Tim's shipped Word Sweep validated the drag-to-trace word mechanic + compact embedded English/Russian dictionaries on a letter grid, but used a rising-grime TIMER as its only pressure. Spell or Spoil reuses the proven tracing + dictionary scaffolding and swaps the timer for a turn-based spreading-rot contagion.

sources.title: Word + spatial-containment strategy gap (puzzle_sort_match family)
sources.snippet: The 2026-06-03 market-signal scan flagged a high-retention-ceiling word/strategy hybrid over the day's top reflex toy. No word game on Yandex/CrazyGames couples spelling to a spatial containment decision; spreading-contagion (mold/rot) is a proven readable hazard in casual puzzle covers (Merge Dale, Block Dropping Merge object-scale references).

sources.title: No shipped word game uses spatial contagion as its pressure axis
sources.snippet: Across Tim's word games (Word Sweep 111, Word Snake 73, Vowel Hold'em 35) none make WHERE you spell matter. Spell or Spoil's cleanse-by-adjacency rot turns each word into a positional containment choice — the synthesizer's distillation of the market signal into a single novel verb.

synthesizer_brief: Leader brief: build a word game whose pressure is a spreading rot, not a timer — so WHERE the player spells is the decision. Reuse Word Sweep's proven drag-to-trace input + embedded English dictionary scaffolding, but replace the rising-grime timer with a turn-based contagion that creeps one step after every submitted word (and every PASS). Make cleansing legible: a word ON or NEXT TO the mold wipes it, so spelling beside the colony contains it. Seed the opening 6x6 board with an obvious word near the two rot seeds for <15s time-to-first-fun. Escalate across an endless board ladder with staged new content (gold x2 from board 1, bleach power tiles from board 2, faster/denser rot from board 3, spore rot-jump tiles from board 4, long-word bonuses) so a 10-minute run keeps showing something new. UI + tutorial bilingual EN+RU via GF.t(); v1 gameplay dictionary is English (RU gameplay dictionary noted as a future iteration).

alternatives.title: Breach
alternatives.hook: Reverse tower-defense: you are the attackers, carving a path through a fixed defensive grid by spending a wave budget to breach the weakest seam.
alternatives.why_novel: Subtractive/attacker-side TD — runner-up; strategy genre but heavier scope than a one-session word puzzle.

alternatives.title: Topple
alternatives.hook: Reverse-Jenga physics — pull blocks to make a tower fall a target direction onto goals without it collapsing on you.
alternatives.why_novel: Controlled-collapse physics puzzle — runner-up; a physics toy, lower retention ceiling than the word/strategy pick.

alternatives.title: Keep Up
alternatives.hook: Reflex juggling toy — keep an escalating number of objects aloft with taps.
alternatives.why_novel: Pure reflex toy — the day's top reflex candidate, NOT built (lower retention ceiling than the strategy pick).

alternatives.title: Shove Off
alternatives.hook: Physics shove arena — knock rivals off a shrinking platform with momentum bursts.
alternatives.why_novel: Momentum-shove arena — runner-up physics brawler, not built this slot.
